California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 5, 1991
c. Victor Leipzig, Ph. D.
Executive Director
The Bolsa chica conservancy
P. O. Box 3976
Huntington Beach, CA 92605
Re: Your Request for Inforrnal Assistance
Our File No. I -91-12 0
Dear !1r . l,eipz ig :
This Letter is in response to your request for advice
concerning your duties and respons ibi I ities under the conflict-of-
interest irovisions of the Pol-itical Reform Act (the "Act").1
Your request does not address one particular decision before your
agency but rather seeks general guidance. Accordingly, we treat
your tetter as a request for informal assistance pursuant to the
irovisions of Regulttion L8329 (copy enclosed) .2
OUESTIONS
1. Do the conflict-of- interest provisions of the Act
disqualify you from participating in decisions when the Bolsa
Chica Foundatj-on is directly before the pl-anning commission?
2. 
Do the confl ict-of- interest provisions of the Act
disqualify you from parti.cipating 1n decisions affecting the Ko11
company which has rnade significant financial contributions to the
BoIsa Chica conservancy?
3. Do the conflict-of- interest provisions of the Act
disqualify you from participating in decisions affecting firms or
1 Gor"..,."nt code sections 81ooo-91015. AIl statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwi-se indicated. commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seq. AIl references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of ReguLations.
2 Inf orrnal, assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Sec-
tion 83114,' Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J street, Suite 8oo . P.o. Box 80? . sacramento cA 958o4-o8oz . (916)322-5660


our File No. I-91- 12 O
Page No. 2
individuals who have made smaller financial donations to the Bolsa
chica conservancy?
CONCLUSIONS
1. The conflict-of- interest provisions of the Act disqualify
you from participating in decisions which will dj.rectly or
indirectly affect the Bolsa Chica Conservancy.
2 and 3. You will not be disqualified fron participating in
decisions affecting individuals or entities whj.ch have nade
donations to the Bolsa Chica Conservancy unless the decision will
have a material- financial effect on the conservancy.
rACTS
You are a planning commissioner for the City of Huntington
Beach. You al-so serve as the executive director of the Bol-sa
chica conservancy (the rrconservancyrr ) . The conservancy is a
nonprofit corporation which conducts environmental research and
education programs. You receive compensation from the conservancy
for the discharge of your duties as a part-time employee. The
conservancy does not own land and it does not engage in commercial
activities.
Local, firms and individuals have made donations to the
conservancy. You have stated in your request for advice that such
contrj,butions indirectly affect you financially. 
Your salary is
not paid to you directly by the KoII ConPany. Hor,/ever, the KoIl"
company has made significant financial contributions to the
conservancy. The Kol,l- conpany is the primary land owner i'n the
Bolsa chica area and engages in land development. An employee of
this company sits on the board of directors of the conservancy.
You expe-t that the KolI company will appear before the planning
commission during your term of office. 
other firms and
individuals also make smaLler financial contributions to the
conservancy .
ANALYSTS
Section 871o0 prohibits public officiats 
from rnaking,
participating i-n making, or in any way usinq their official
position to infLuence a governmental decision in which they have a
financial, interest. 
A planning cornrnissioner is a public official'
(Section 82048. )
A public officiaL has a financial interest in a decision if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision wilf have a


our File No. I-91-12 o
Page No. 3
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public AeneraIIy. on the official, 
on a menber of the official's
immediate familyr or on:
Any source of income, other than gifts and
other ttran Loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terns available to the public without reqard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dol-Iars ($2s01 or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the tine when the
decision is rnade.
Section 87103 (c) .
Your facts indicate that the conservancy is a source of
incorne to you. For purposes of our analysis we assume that this
incorne exceeds S25o in a 12-month period. Accordingly, you must
disqualify yourself from participating in decisions which wiII
foreseeabl,y and materially affect the conservancy.
Regulation 18702.l- (copy enclosed) provides that the.effect
of a de6ision is rnaterial if a source of income to a public
official 
of $250 or more in the preceding 12 nonths is directly
involved in a decision before the official's 
agency or there is a
nexus between the purpose for which the official 
receives income
and the governrnentil decision. A source of income to a public
official 
is directly involved in a decision before an official's
agency i.\rhen that person or entity, either personally or by an
agent :
(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the
decision will be made by filing an application,
claim, appeal,, or sj-milar request or;
(2) Is a naned party in, or is the subject
of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official 
or the official's 
agency.
(3) A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceedinq if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approvat, denial or revocation
of any J-icense, permj-t, or other entitlernent to, or
contract with, the subject person or business
entity.
Regulation 18702.1(b)
3 An official-'s 
rtimmediate farniLyrt consists of his or her spouse
and dependent children. 
(section 82029.)


our Fil-e No. I-91- L2 0
Page No. 4
As you correctly state in your request for advice, if the
conservancy appeared before the planning cornmission, you would
have to disquatify yourself from participating in such decisions.
You hrould also be disqualified from participating J-n decisions
rrhere there is a nexus bet$reen your position as executive director
of the conservancy and your official duties i.n the planning
cornmission. This $/ould be the case, for example, if the planning
conmission endorsed a particular itern of environmental research
prepared by the conservancy. This would also be the case if the
conlervancy srere to take a position on a matter pending before the
planning commission. (Freers Advice Letter, No. A-86-281, copy
enc Iosed . )
Your disqual i fication $/ould also be required if the
conservancy were affected indirectly by the decision. This would
be the case, for example, if a decision pending before the
planning commission would affect a real property interest held by
the conservancy, such as a lease.
when a nonprofit entity is indirectLy affected by a decision,
the standard foi deterrni,ning nateriality is that of Regulation
t87o2.5 (copy enclosed) . For exanple, if the nonprofit has gross
annual- r"ceipts of $100,000 or less, the effect of a decision will
be material if:
(1) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease of the entity's gross annual receipts
for a fiscal year in the amount of $10,000 or nore.
(2) The decision will cause the entity to
incur or avoid additional expenses or to reduce or
el.iminate existing expenses for a fiscal year in
the amount of 92,500 or more.
(3) The decision will result in an increase
or decrease in the value of the entity's assets or
liabil,ities 
in the amount of $10,000 or more.
Regulation 18702.5 (f ) .
Therefore, if you determine that a decision before the
planning commission will indirectly affect the conservancy, your
disqual if ication is required if the decision e/iI1 affect the
conservancy in the amounts indicated above.
You have also asked whether your disqual ification 
J-s required
when an indlvidual or a business entity which has made a gift to
the conservancy appears before the planning cornmission. As a


our File No. r-91-12 0
Page No. 5
general rule, donors to the nonprofit conservancy e/ouLd not be
sources of income to you. (Dean Advice Letter, No. A-89-559, copy
encl-osed.) However, your disqual ification would be required if it
is foreseeable that a decision affecting a source of gifts to the
conservancy would result in an increase or decrease in the incorne
of the conlervancy by the amounts set forth in the appJ-icable
provisions of Regulation 18702.5, discussed above. For example,
lou have indicated that the Ko1l conpany is a najor. contributor to
th" con="rvan"y. rf it is foreseeable that a decision affecting
Ko11 conpany night result j-n a material reduction in the funding
of the conservancy, then your disqualification wouLd be required-
otherwise, as stated above, individuals or entities which make
donations to the conservancy are not disqualifying sources of
income to you.
we trust this l-etter adequately responds to your inquiry-
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call- ne at (916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counseL

By: Bl,anca !1. Breeze
counsel, Legal Division
SH:BMB:dg
Enclosures
